definitions.gloss_en: a stat representing quickness, dexterity, or reflexes; may affect initiative, dodge, or movement
definitions.gloss_jp: {すばやさ|すばやさ}や{反|はん}{射|しゃ}{神|しん}{経|けい}を{示|しめ}す{ステータス|すてーたす}；{先|せん}{攻|こう}{権|けん}や{回|かい}{避|ひ}に{影|えい}{響|きょう}することがある
definitions.explanation_en: A TRPG-standard stat for Dexterity/Agility. Governs movement speed, reaction time, and evasion. In wargames, may affect which side acts first.
definitions.implicit_notes: 敏捷性 is the established Japanese TRPG rendering of Dexterity (D&D, Pathfinder). The docx notes that 速度 (speed) is incorrect for this concept — 速度 means rate of motion, not reflexive quickness.
definitions.orthography_notes: 敏捷: 敏 (quick/nimble) + 捷 (agile/swift). 性 (sei) as a suffix: quality/nature. 捷 (U+6377) is a less-common kanji but standard in 敏捷. Goo dictionary lists 敏捷 as a general adjective. Hobby Japan D&D translation uses 敏捷性 for Dexterity. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: TRPG-standard. Hobby Japan D&D and Pathfinder translations both use 敏捷性 for the Dexterity/Agility ability score. In wargames, used for initiative and evasion stats.

usage_jp: {敏|びん}{捷|しょう}{性|せい}ステータス (Agility stat); {敏|びん}{捷|しょう}{性|せい}判{定|てい} (Agility check, TRPG)
comparative_notes: 速度 (sokudo, speed) is incorrect — it measures rate of motion, not reflexive quickness. 敏捷性 is the established TRPG standard for Dexterity/Agility.

examples.japanese: {敏|びん}{捷|しょう}{性|せい}が{高|たか}いユニットは{先|せん}{攻|こう}できる。

cross_references.note: 力 — Strength stat (paired ability score in TRPG contexts)
